rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Dated-session narration (2026-04-24) is the deliberate framed-memo convention, not a stale marker; no 'NOTE: outdated' / 'fixed' / 'MOVED' markers in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 quintet; M6 mood (unleashed leverage) Risto-led — body is Risto epistemic directness + Boris-flat factual. No atmospheric-phrasing or negation-callout self-contradiction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Antti signature at close (L67) and first-person 'we'/'our' are the explicit framed-memoir exceptions named in this rule for story-of-module-6.md; framing line L3 sets the memo convention.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'you' = student throughout; trainer-read note for L3 framing line lives below the maintainer cut.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: This is the lecture, not the reference artefact; the verbatim frame is captured in the maintainer block + session-notes source.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Geoff Charles cited once in body (L29).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Punchy line 'A rule in context is not a rule in the output. Taste closes the gap.' (L45) holds; no hedged-middle softening, boundary carried by surrounding memo.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'we live what we teach' / 'the method scales to messy work' (L23) appear ONLY as the rejected framing Antti cut — narrated as a negative example, not imported as a teaching beat. 'vendor' absent from body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M6 is end-of-arc; subagents, verifier, sim, eval, source-verify all earned upstream.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture memo, no exercise forcing function being trimmed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture memo, not an exercise '**What happened:**' recap section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'You will catch what I missed' / 'Your turn' — aspirational; no 'you already think' / 'you're missing'; no ability-tier pegging.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M6 end-of-arc; subagents/verifier/sim/eval/compounding all landed earlier in arc; bare 'M5'/'M6' fine post-M2.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 software-engineer audience, not Agents 101 business default.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'lived in memory but never fired' (L49) and 'training-data memory' (L31) precise; 'loaded into context' (L21) correct for runtime context; no 'context compounds' blur.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No prompts in this lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture, not a Debrief / compound section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Memo narrates Antti catching drift and then encoding it into skills that block 'done'; teaches encode-the-check, not student-as-manual-error-catcher. No student ask.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: In-room lecture; sims narrated are generation-time sims, not a self-study simulation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Charles cited inline (L29); no callout box.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture has no student moves / prompts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No CLAUDE.md reference in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture section headers are declarative noun-phrases ('The numbers', 'Things that went sideways', 'What the loop is for') — rule 17 exempts lectures (reader's job = understanding).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No inline links in body; the module-design path is in the maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'memory' used distinctly ('lived in memory' = rules/skills; 'training-data memory'); M6 is well past first authored write, disambiguation earned upstream; no first-authored write here needing a fresh line.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body Claude:agent = 22:1, but 'the LLM' used 8x and correctly reserved for cognition/drift ('hold something uncomfortable about LLMs' L9, 'the LLM did not find it' L23, 'LLMs drift' L45). 'Claude' = the specific instance Antti addressed/steered through the session (product/addressee, rule-21-allowed). Discrimination is deliberate and correct for a memoir narrating a steering session, not the assistant-shaped 10:0 the rule warns of.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'the cohort would read on Monday' (L31) is a weekday, not on rule 22's relative-time-of-day banned list (tomorrow/overnight/this morning/tonight/yesterday/last night); no banned anchors present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'Meta-moment' / 'Step back' / 'sit with' / 'breathe' headers or prefixes; 'Your turn.' is a natural close, not a pedagogy-aware label.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No prompts / meta-asks in lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No artifact-saving prompts; referenced files (plan.md, ramp.md) already .md.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Dense memo; each fail-beat advances the non-determinism thesis. No implementation-detail-before-use or administrative aside in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'You will catch what I missed' (L43), 'The next hour you will diagnose... You will author... You will ship it.' (L57), 'Your turn.' (L65) — student is subject of declarative future claims; no training-as-subject or role-gating.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture, not a module file / exercise with per-module operational settings (thinking-effort, model, mode tag).
